domain: Model Routing, Cascading & Orchestration for SLMs

categories.intelligent_routers: Tools that analyze prompts and select the best model per-request based on quality, cost, latency
categories.ai_gateways: Unified API layers with routing, failover, load balancing, and observability

tools.name: Not Diamond
tools.description: AI model router that predicts the best-suited foundation model per-request. Recommender-only architecture (not a proxy). Includes prompt optimization and agent optimization (beta).
tools.models_supported: GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Llama 3.1, Gemini, etc.
tools.strengths: Up to 25% accuracy improvement over individual LLMs, up to 10x cost reduction
tools.weaknesses: Custom pricing only, limited transparency on methodology

tools.name: OpenRouter
tools.description: Unified API providing access to 500+ models from 60+ providers through a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Automatic failover and intelligent routing.
tools.funding: $40M (Seed + Series A, a16z + Menlo Ventures, June 2025)
tools.strengths: Largest model catalog, strong developer adoption, well-funded
tools.weaknesses: Primarily a proxy/aggregator rather than intelligent quality-based router

tools.name: RouteLLM
tools.description: Framework from LMSYS for serving and evaluating LLM routers. Routes simpler queries to cheaper models using trained classifiers on human preference data.
key_features: Trained routers on human preference data
tools.strengths: Strong academic backing, proven cost savings, self-hosted
tools.weaknesses: Requires training your own routers, limited to strong vs weak binary routing

tools.name: LLMRouter (ulab-uiuc)
tools.description: Open-source library with 16+ routing models across four categories: single-round, multi-round, agentic, and personalized routers. Unified CLI and plugin system.
tools.strengths: Most comprehensive open-source router library, multiple routing paradigms
tools.weaknesses: Relatively new, smaller community than RouteLLM

tools.name: Martian
tools.description: Decision engine that sits on top of multiple providers and chooses models automatically per-prompt. Uses internal model behavior analysis for routing decisions.
tools.strengths: Deep model behavior understanding, prompt-aware routing
tools.weaknesses: Less transparent methodology, smaller ecosystem

tools.name: Unify.ai
tools.description: Customizable LLMOps platform using live benchmarks and dynamic policies to optimize for cost/speed/quality routing.
tools.strengths: Data-driven routing with live benchmarks, highly customizable
tools.weaknesses: Managed service dependency, less control over routing logic

tools.name: Portkey AI
tools.description: Enterprise-grade AI gateway with routing to 1600+ LLMs. Supports fallback, load balancing, conditional routing, canary deployments, and circuit breakers.
tools.strengths: Broadest provider support, enterprise governance, open-source gateway
tools.weaknesses: Gateway overhead, complexity for simple use cases

tools.name: LiteLLM
tools.description: Python SDK and proxy server (AI Gateway) to call 100+ LLM APIs in OpenAI format. Auto routing with embedding-based utterance matching.
tools.strengths: Widely adopted, simple setup, comprehensive provider support
tools.weaknesses: Auto routing is basic (utterance matching), not quality-aware

tools.name: Helicone
tools.description: Open-source LLM observability platform with AI Gateway. Cost-based routing, BYOK priority, semantic caching, and load balancing.
tools.strengths: Best-in-class observability, cost transparency, open source
tools.weaknesses: Routing is primarily cost/latency-based, not quality-aware

tools.name: NVIDIA LLM Router
tools.description: NVIDIA blueprint for routing LLM requests to the best model. Uses CLIP embeddings and trained neural networks to predict optimal models based on quality, latency, and cost.
tools.strengths: NVIDIA backing, embedding-based approach, multi-objective optimization
tools.weaknesses: Tightly coupled to NVIDIA ecosystem, blueprint (not production-ready)

tools.name: vLLM Semantic Router
tools.description: System-level intelligent router for Mixture-of-Models (MoM). Extracts signals from requests for routing, safety, caching, and hallucination detection. Envoy External Processor architecture.
tools.strengths: Most architecturally advanced OSS router, safety features, vLLM ecosystem
tools.weaknesses: Complex setup, early-stage project

tools.name: NadirClaw
tools.description: Open-source LLM router and AI cost optimizer. Routes simple prompts to cheap/local models and complex ones to premium models.
tools.strengths: Simple concept, local model support
tools.weaknesses: Less sophisticated routing, smaller community

tools.name: ClawRouter
tools.description: Agent-native LLM router. 41+ models, <1ms routing, USDC payments on Base & Solana.
tools.strengths: Ultra-fast routing, multi-dimensional analysis
tools.weaknesses: Crypto-focused niche, smaller adoption

tools.name: Requesty
tools.description: Unified LLM gateway with intelligent routing that auto-selects the cheapest viable model meeting quality bars. Enterprise governance with PII detection, RBAC, and budget controls.
tools.strengths: Enterprise-ready, strong governance, fast failover
tools.weaknesses: Managed service lock-in, newer entrant

tools.name: Bifrost (Maxim AI)
tools.description: High-performance open-source LLM gateway built in Go. 11 microsecond gateway overhead at 5000 RPS. OpenAI-compatible API for 20+ providers.
tools.strengths: Fastest gateway (11μs overhead), Go performance, enterprise governance
tools.weaknesses: Limited intelligent routing (primarily load balancing)

tools.name: TrueFoundry AI Gateway
tools.description: Enterprise AI gateway with weight-based and latency-based routing. Real-time health monitoring with automatic unhealthy model exclusion.
tools.strengths: Enterprise-grade, geo-aware routing, strong monitoring
tools.weaknesses: Less focus on quality-based intelligent routing

tools.name: Anyscale LLM Router
tools.description: Tutorial and reference implementation for building LLM routers. Demonstrates building high-quality, cost-effective routing systems.
tools.strengths: Educational, well-documented approach
tools.weaknesses: Tutorial only, not production-ready

tools.name: MixLLM
tools.description: Dynamic routing in mixed large language models. Research paper from NAACL 2025 on optimal model mixing strategies.
tools.strengths: Theoretical foundations for optimal routing
tools.weaknesses: Academic only, no production implementation

tools.name: Speculative Cascades (Google Research)
tools.description: Hybrid approach combining cascading and speculative decoding. Small model decides if it can handle query or needs to defer to larger model.
key_features: Deferral rule from small to large
Better quality at lower compute cost
tools.strengths: Best of cascading + speculative decoding, Google backing
tools.weaknesses: Not publicly available, Google-internal

tools.name: Amazon Bedrock Intelligent Prompt Routing
tools.description: AWS's built-in prompt routing within Bedrock that routes between models in the same family based on complexity.
tools.strengths: Native AWS integration, no extra infrastructure
tools.weaknesses: Limited to Bedrock model families, not cross-provider

market_overview.market_trend: Rapid consolidation around gateway + routing pattern. Moving from simple load-balancing to quality-aware intelligent routing.
key_research_papers: Dynamic Model Routing and Cascading Survey (2026, arXiv:2603.04445)
Unified Approach to Routing and Cascading (OpenReview)
market_overview.energy_efficiency_note: Context-aware model switching can reduce energy consumption by up to 67.5% while maintaining 93.6% response quality
